title: Use Containerfile by default
body:
Use the file named "Containerfile" if no build file is specified.  If
not found, fall back to Dockerfile.

This is the initial PR, we probably will have more doc tweaking to do overtime.

```
# cd /tmp
# cp ~/Dockerfile.alpine ./Containerfile
# buildah bud -t tom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: COMMIT tom
961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4

# mv ./Containerfile ./Dockerfile
# buildah bud -t tom .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: COMMIT tom
961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4

```
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I began to play with this on Friday and over the weekend.  LGTM.
But I did fall into a bunch of docs issues and us using the letters "d o c k e r" all over the place.  I think keeping it simple to start and updating the man pages is the way to go.
I've added a text and touched up the bud page.  Probably other places that we can tweak overtime, but think this works for the first effort.
I think you need to make sure you have examples with Dockerfile and Containerfile, in the man page.

@vrothberg I've added a new "Dockerfile" test too.  I'd not done that earlier as we do that in 95% of the tests, but probably good to have one specifically.  Holler if I misinterpreted your suggestion.

> @vrothberg I've added a new "Dockerfile" test too. I'd not done that earlier as we do that in 95% of the tests, but probably good to have one specifically. Holler if I misinterpreted your suggestion.

Thanks, that was it :) I just came to think of another test using a directory as context which containers a Dockerfile and a Containerfile. The build should then select the Containerfile. It won't increase coverage but could prevent potential regressions in the future.
@vrothberg, good thought, just added another test.

:pushpin: Commit d6f7305 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan d6f73055a33c2aa782f67c53ca3bad73cb6d8d10 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
The status of this PR is closed, did it get merged, or closed out automatically by rh-atomic-bot?

It's merged.  Buildah now uses files named "Containerfiles" and if not present, then "Dockerfiles" before erroring.  Syntax internally is the same between both files.  Not yet in Podman, but should be the next time Buildah gets vendored there.
Just to be clear, that's just in upstream ATM, will be in v1.11.3 or v1.12.0, whichever the next release is.
